method_atom: A construction of a multiplicative, symmetry-equivariant structure-preserving map (an orientation/genus) between two ring-valued graded invariants that are required to respect a continuous group action: one invariant arising from a universal classifying construction and the other built from the geometry of an algebraic curve. The map is pinned down and computed via a theory of characteristic classes, given a conceptual description in terms of algebraic geometry, and used to settle a pre-existing conjecture.
object_atom: Circle-equivariant stable homotopy theory: the circle-equivariant cobordism spectrum MString_C (equivariant analogue of MU<6> for stably almost complex manifolds with c_1=c_2=0), the T-equivariant elliptic cohomology ring spectrum EC associated to a rational/complex elliptic curve, and the sigma orientation of Ando-Hopkins-Strickland.
brief_reason: The transferable part is building an equivariant multiplicative map between two ring-valued invariants (one universal/classifying, one from an algebraic curve) verified by characteristic classes; the domain-specific object is the concrete equivariant spectra MString_C and elliptic-cohomology EC, though the machinery is somewhat fused to stable-homotopy-theoretic objects.
